title: Nestlé India Expands Maggi Noodles Production with New Line at Sanand Factory - scanx.trade
content:
Nestlé India Expands Maggi Noodles Production with New Line at Sanand Factory
Nestlé India Limited announced the addition of a new Maggi Noodles production line at its Sanand Factory in Gujarat, adding 20,500 tons per annum capacity with an investment of ₹90 crores funded through internal accruals. The expansion is scheduled for completion during Financial Year 2026-27 and is part of the company's planned capital expenditure to meet future demand. The Sanand Factory currently has existing capacity of 141,600 tons per annum with 80% utilization rate.
*this image is generated using AI for illustrative purposes only.
Nestlé India Limited has announced the addition of a new Maggi Noodles production line at its Sanand Factory in Gujarat, marking a significant expansion in its manufacturing capabilities. The company disclosed this development through a regulatory filing under Regulation 30 of the SEBI Listing Regulations.
Production Capacity Enhancement
The new production line will substantially boost the company's Maggi Noodles manufacturing capacity at the Sanand facility. The expansion represents a strategic move to strengthen the company's position in the instant noodles market.
Investment and Financing
The capacity expansion project requires a total investment of ₹90 crores (approx.), which will be funded entirely through internal accruals. This financing approach demonstrates the company's strong cash generation capabilities and commitment to self-funded growth initiatives.
Strategic Rationale
The company stated that this capacity addition forms part of its planned capital expenditure in both greenfield and brownfield projects aimed at increasing overall manufacturing capacities. The expansion is specifically designed to meet anticipated future demand for Maggi Noodles products. Nestlé India also manufactures Maggi Noodles at other factories within its production network, indicating a comprehensive approach to capacity management across multiple locations.
The Sanand Factory currently operates at approximately 80% capacity utilization, suggesting room for growth in existing operations alongside the new production line addition. This expansion will enable the company to better serve market demand while maintaining operational efficiency across its manufacturing network.
